judgment.summary: Home Depot shows strong profitability and returns, but faces headwinds from declining revenue and high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: For the quarter ending January 31, 2026, Home Depot reported an EPS of $2.72, beating the estimate of $2.52 by 7.8%. The next earnings date is August 18, 2026.
positives: Profit margin of 8.6% and Return on Equity of 145.5% indicate strong profitability and efficient use of shareholder capital.
Free cash flow of $8.62B provides substantial operational cash generation.
Dividend yield of 3.1% offers an attractive income component for investors.
Analyst consensus is a 'buy' with a mean target price of $395.48, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Revenue growth of -3.8% and Earnings growth of -14.2% indicate a recent downturn in top and bottom line performance.
Debt/Equity ratio of 514.39% is very high, suggesting significant financial leverage.
Quick ratio of 0.21 indicates potential short-term liquidity challenges.
Forward P/E of 18.73x and PEG ratio of 2.74x suggest the stock is priced for growth that is not currently materializing.

news.paragraph: Home Depot's stock is currently trading around $302.44 amidst a flurry of analyst price target adjustments following a mixed earnings environment in the home improvement sector, exemplified by Lowe's performance. While several analysts maintain outperform or buy ratings, many have lowered their price targets, reflecting a cautious outlook on the stock's near-term potential. Traders should consider the divergence in analyst sentiment and the overall sector performance when evaluating options contracts.

headlines.title: Lowe’s Posts Earnings Beat. Why the Stock Is Falling.
Truist Securities Adjusts Price Target on Home Depot to $369 From $394, Maintains Buy Rating
RBC Cuts Price Target on Home Depot to $340 From $377, Keeps Sector Perform Rating

Baird Adjusts Price Target on Home Depot to $380 From $430, Maintains Outperform Rating
Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on Home Depot to $385 From $415, Maintains Outperform Rating
BNP Paribas Adjusts Price Target on Home Depot to $325 From $348, Maintains Neutral Rating
Telsey Advisory Adjusts PT on Home Depot to $410 From $435, Maintains Outperform Rating
JPMorgan Adjusts Price Target on Home Depot to $396 From $423, Maintains Overweight Rating
